why_it_matters: Reasoning claims can change across model snapshots, distilled variants, and product SKUs.
GOAL.md cutoff requires knowing whether the source predates May 24, 2026 and whether it describes a future roadmap or current release.
Reasoning gains are task-specific; a math result, code result, or chat preference row cannot become all-domain ability.
SWE-bench Verified, Terminal-bench, AIME year, GPQA variant, or dataset split changes the claim scope.
Tools, browsing, terminals, code execution, verifiers, and agents make the benchmark a harness result, not raw-model proof.
applies_to: o-series, ReAct, SWE-bench, LiveCodeBench, Claude/agent claims

why_it_matters: Self-consistency, pass@k, candidate generation, and repeated attempts spend inference differently.
applies_to: self-consistency, coding benchmarks, verifiers, R1/Kimi, o-series

why_it_matters: Hidden or visible extra thinking changes cost, latency, and comparability.
Verifier, majority vote, tree search, unit tests, or human preference determines what success means.
Benchmark leakage and post-release task inclusion can turn a score into a bad proxy.
Vendor launch numbers are useful but cannot be narrated as neutral crowns.
applies_to: OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, Kimi, Gemini, xAI/lab posts

why_it_matters: Visible chain text is not necessarily faithful hidden reasoning, and hidden chains are not accessible unless the source says so.
Cost or latency claims require same-scope pricing/runtime evidence, not just a reasoning-method paper.
applies_to: o-series, self-consistency, tree search, economics chapter
